deepseek-r1-distill-llama-8b.text: Okay, so I'm trying to figure out the diagnosis for this 55-year-old male with obstructive jaundice and a 7 kg weight loss.

The pancreas itself looks normal, so it's unlikely to be a pancreatic issue like pancreatitis or a tumor.
The weight loss is significant—7 kg in two months.
That's a red flag for malignancy because weight loss is a common symptom in cancers, especially in the context of obstructive jaundice.
Cancers that can cause this might include cholangiocarcinoma, which affects the bile ducts.
Looking at the CT findings, the CBD dilation suggests a distal obstruction.
Cholangiocarcinoma, especially extrahepatic, can cause this.
The main pancreatic duct dilation might be due to the same underlying condition.
Since the pancreas is normal, it's not a pancreatic head or body tumor but more likely a distal obstruction.
So, putting it all together, the most likely diagnosis is extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma.
This makes sense because it explains the obstructive jaundice and the weight loss.
